candidates.evidence_quote: Provisions in our certificate of incorporation and bylaws and Delaware law may discourage, delay or prevent a change of control of our Company or changes in our management and, therefore, may depress the trading price of our stock

Significant legal fees and costs in complex class action litigation or an adverse judgment or settlement that is not insured or is in excess of insurance coverage could have a material adverse effect on our financial position and results of operations
We are also subject to the risk of being named a joint employer of workers of our franchisees for alleged violations of labor and wage laws
severe winter weather conditions, wildfires and hurricanes have impacted our traffic, our franchisees, and results of operations in recent years
If a regional or global health pandemic occurs, depending upon its location, duration and severity, our business could be severely affected
consumer traffic may decline due to the actual or perceived effects from these events
disrupting or delaying production and delivery of materials and products in our supply chain
causing staffing shortages in our restaurants

This increased focus could lead to legislative, regulatory or other efforts to combat these environmental concerns. These efforts could result in further increases in taxes, cost of supplies, transportation and utilities, which could increase our operating costs
if our insurance costs increase, there can be no assurance that we will be able to successfully offset the effect of such increases and our results of operations may be adversely affected
We have experienced impairment of such assets recently, and identified others where the fair value has become closer to the carrying value

A significant financial reporting failure or a lack of sufficient internal control over financial reporting could cause a loss of investor confidence and decline in the market price of our common stock

Future changes to existing accounting rules or standards, new pronouncements and varying interpretations of pronouncements, or the questioning of current accounting practices may adversely affect our reported financial results
the effects of such events on economic conditions and consumer spending patterns, could disrupt our operations or supply chain and negatively impact our results of operations
we cannot be certain that our internal control over financial reporting and disclosure controls and procedures will prevent all possible errors and fraud, including through cyber-attacks

notes: Chunk contains a single event: 10-K accession 0001546417-26-000009 (2026-02-25), part 5 of 5 - tail of Risk Factors (General Risk Factors) only; no fiscal_kpis field present in this chunk.

Skipped (vague or not a reusable cause): (1) execution of disaster-recovery/business-continuity plans at the Tampa Restaurant Support Center (operational contingency, not a specific reusable causal variable); (2) terrorist attacks / war (passing list items inside the disaster enumeration, no specific narrative); (3) sub-types of consumer/employee claims (food-borne illness, dram shop, premises liability, discrimination) folded under litigation_costs rather than coined separately.
Split one sentence into two drivers per one-variable-per-name rule: supply_chain_disruption and restaurant_staffing (pandemic paragraph names both supply delivery delays and staffing shortages).
All evidence_quote strings verified verbatim (contiguous substrings) against the event content before writing this menu.
